---
title: "Exercise 1 — GitHub Account & First Repository"
format: html
---

**Module 1 | ~20 minutes**

---

## Goal

Create your GitHub account and your first repository.

---

## Steps

### GitHub Account

1. Go to [https://github.com](https://github.com) and sign up for a free account
2. Choose a professional username — you will share this publicly
3. Verify your email address

### Explore GitHub

4. Go to your profile page: `https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME`
5. Find the **DQBM-SIB** organization — check if you are already a member

### Create a Repository

6. Click the **+** icon (top right) → **New repository**
7. Name it exactly your GitHub username (e.g., `dktanwar`)
8. Set it to **Public**
9. Check **Add a README file**
10. Click **Create repository**

### Verify

11. Go to `https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/YOUR_USERNAME`
12. You should see your new repository with a README file
13. Take a screenshot — we will compare it after Exercise 2

---

## What you learned

- How GitHub is organized: users, organizations, repositories
- The difference between public and private repositories
- A README file is the front page of your repository
